mainEntity.name: How many retaining wall blocks do I need?

acceptedAnswer.text: Divide the wall area (length × height) by the face area of one block. For 6" gravity blocks, plan about 2.25 blocks per square foot. For 12" reinforced blocks, plan about 1.125 blocks per square foot. Always add 5-10% for waste.

mainEntity.name: How much gravel backfill do I need for a retaining wall?

acceptedAnswer.text: A common rule is 1 cubic yard of gravel backfill per 12-15 linear feet of wall at 3 feet high. The backfill zone is typically 12 inches deep behind the wall. Our calculator uses wall dimensions and a 12-inch gravel zone to estimate cubic yards.

mainEntity.name: Do I need base gravel for a retaining wall?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Every retaining wall needs a compacted gravel base at least 6 inches deep and extending 2 inches past each side of the block. This provides drainage and a level foundation. Use crushed angular gravel, not round river rock.

mainEntity.name: How much adhesive do I need for retaining wall blocks?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use landscape block adhesive on the cap row and every 2-3 courses on taller walls. One 10 oz tube covers roughly 20-25 linear feet. For cap blocks alone, divide the wall length by 20 for the number of tubes needed.

mainEntity.name: What type of retaining wall block should I use?

acceptedAnswer.text: For walls under 2 feet, 6" gravity blocks are sufficient. Walls 2-4 feet use 8" gravity blocks. Walls over 4 feet typically require 12" reinforced blocks with rebar and concrete fill. Always check local building codes for walls over 4 feet, as permits and engineering may be required.

mainEntity.name: How much does a retaining wall cost per linear foot?

acceptedAnswer.text: Materials typically cost $15-$30 per square foot of wall face for gravity block walls and $25-$50 for reinforced walls. A 3-foot-high, 20-foot-long wall (60 sq ft) runs roughly $900-$1,800 in materials. Labor can double the total cost.

howItWorks:
How to Calculate Retaining Wall Materials

The calculator determines blocks needed from wall area and block face size, then estimates backfill gravel, base gravel, cap blocks, and adhesive.

Blocks = (Wall Length × Height) × Blocks/SqFt × 1.05
  • Wall blocks: face area divided by single block face area, plus 5% waste
  • Cap blocks: wall length ÷ cap block length (~18" = 1.5 ft)
  • Backfill gravel: wall length × wall height × 1 ft depth ÷ 27 cu ft/yd
  • Base gravel: wall length × block depth × 0.5 ft depth ÷ 27
  • Adhesive: 1 tube per 20 linear feet for cap row
faqHTML:
Frequently Asked Questions

How many retaining wall blocks do I need?

Divide the wall face area (length × height) by the face area of one block. For 6" gravity blocks at 18"×6", that's about 1.33 blocks per sq ft. Add 5-10% for waste and cuts.

How much gravel backfill do I need?

Plan for a 12-inch gravel zone behind the wall. Multiply wall length × height × 1 ft, then divide by 27 to convert to cubic yards. For a 20 ft × 3 ft wall: 20 × 3 × 1 ÷ 27 = 2.2 cu yd.

Do I need base gravel?

Yes — a 6-inch compacted gravel base is essential. Use crushed angular gravel for stability. The base should extend 2 inches past each side of the block.

How much adhesive do I need?

One 10 oz tube of landscape block adhesive covers about 20 linear feet. Use it on the cap row and every 2-3 courses on taller walls.

What block type should I use?

6" gravity blocks for walls under 2 ft, 8" gravity blocks for 2-4 ft, and 12" reinforced blocks for walls over 4 ft. Walls over 4 ft often require a building permit and engineering.

How much does a retaining wall cost?

Materials run $15-$30 per sq ft of wall face for gravity blocks and $25-$50 for reinforced. A 3 ft × 20 ft wall typically costs $900-$1,800 in materials alone.

sections.title: What Is the Retaining Wall Calculator?
sections.body: The Retaining Wall Calculator is a free online tool designed for contractors and DIY builders who need quick, accurate calculations in the construction and building space. By entering your wall length, wall height, block type, you get instant results including wall blocks, cap blocks, adhesive tubes. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting wall blocks right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In construction and building, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by contractors and DIY builders worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to plan your projects with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your wall length and need to find the right wall blocks. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Wall Length (ft)

howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: wall height, block type, block price. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your wall blocks, cap blocks, adhesive tubes. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your wall length before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.

faq.question: Is the Retaining Wall Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard construction and building formulas trusted by contractors and DIY builders. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What wall length should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate wall length value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the Retaining Wall Calculator is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

faq.question: What does wall blocks mean in my results?
faq.answer: The wall blocks value represents the primary output of the calculation based on your inputs. Use it as a baseline figure for your construction and building planning and decision-making.
